calibration_boundary: The cases are realistic owner-generated calibration cases. They close live-case calibration evidence, but not qualified expert review.
expected_behavior: Cases should preserve evidence, inference, uncertainty, failure mode, and next-action boundaries.
observed_behavior: Cases were routed into structured calibration fields with explicit limits and reviewer-needed language.

calibration_drift.description: Future changes to public page copy or instrument language could drift away from the calibration assumptions unless rerun after each material edit.

false_positive_risk: The workflow may mark a weakly supported public claim as ready if source ceilings are not read alongside the calibration record.
false_negative_risk: The workflow may reject useful exploratory claims too early if all incomplete evidence is treated as failure rather than as pending research.
brittle_behavior: The calibration is text-structured; stronger operation will require executable page-specific instruments or independent reviewer case trials.
